creator: leres@freebsd.org
text:
Turning off the MANPAGES option generates lots of errors
	on install and fatal errors when attempting to create a
	package.

Fix: Wrap remaining MAN5 and MAN8 assignments with a
	".if ${PORT_OPTIONS:MMANPAGES}" test; see attached patch.
How-To-Repeat: 	(With MANPAGES disasbled)

	# cd /usr/ports/net-mgmt/argus3 && make install
	[...]
	===>   Compressing manual pages for argus-sasl-3.0.6.1
	gzip: can't stat: /usr/local/man/man5/argus.conf.5: No such file or directory
	gzip: can't stat: /usr/local/man/man8/argus.8: No such file or directory
	[...]
	# make package
	bob2 194 # make package
	===>  Building package for argus-sasl-3.0.6.1
	tar: man/man5/argus.conf.5.gz: Cannot stat: No such file or directory
	tar: man/man8/argus.8.gz: Cannot stat: No such file or directory
	tar: Error exit delayed from previous errors.
	pkg_create: make_dist: tar command failed with code 256
	*** Error code 1
